title: micropiper pusillum
intro: Micropiper pusillum, a small, annual vine found in the tropical regions of Asia, is a member of the Piperaceae family and is commonly known as the small Indian pepper or Pipli. It has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The extracts of this plant have been reported to have various health benefits, including pain relief, wound healing, and antimicrobial activity, making it a potential remedy for various health conditions. Micropiper pusillum is often used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and ointments, and is also used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various ailments.
